old_claim.note: Basic Fermentation is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No direct fermentation edge remains because herbal medicine preparation is scoped to broad plant-remedy processing; fermented remedies are one possible subtype, not a prerequisite for poultices, teas, oils, resins, or direct plant preparations.

source_shape.source_locator: Historical review of medicinal plant usage: surveys medicinal plants across prehistoric and ancient traditions without making fermentation the prerequisite technology for plant-remedy preparation.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The review supports medicinal-plant use and preparation traditions, but it does not make fermentation necessary for the broad herbal-preparation node.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Fermentation remains a relevant specialized preparation method elsewhere, yet the target node has many dependents that require general plant-remedy preparation rather than fermented medicines.

ontology_before: Herbal Medicine Preparation depended directly on Basic Fermentation, causing all downstream medical-preparation paths to inherit a fermentation prerequisite.
ontology_after: Herbal Medicine Preparation depends on Herbalism and pottery-enabled preparation/storage; fermentation is not modeled as a direct prerequisite for the broad umbrella.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: fermentation_basic is no longer a direct prerequisite for herbal_medicine_preparation.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed specifically to fermented herbal remedies.
A stronger source showed that broad ancient herbal preparation required fermentation rather than crushing, steeping, boiling, poultices, oils, or direct plant use.
A separate fermented-herbal-remedies node were added where fermentation_basic should be the direct prerequisite.
short_reason: Fermentation is a possible subtype, not a direct prerequisite for broad herbal medicine preparation.
